fund.name: BANK OF INDIA Flexi Cap Fund Direct Plan -Growth
fund.fund_house: Bank of India Investment Managers Private Limited

allocations.rationale: High-alpha flexi cap fund with 18.3% alpha and 1.10 Sharpe ratio — demonstrates superior risk-adjusted returns through active stock picking. Fund manager shows conviction in mid/small cap growth stories while maintaining low beta (0.87). Ideal for aggressive investors seeking alpha over market beta.

fund.name: HDFC Mid Cap Fund - Growth Option - Direct Plan

allocations.rationale: Consistent performer in mid cap category with 25% 3-year CAGR and high information ratio (1.85). Low tracking error (7.8%) indicates focused portfolio with genuine stock-picking skill. AUM >₹24,000 Cr ensures stability. Delivers strong growth with controlled volatility.

fund.name: BANDHAN SMALL CAP FUND - DIRECT PLAN GROWTH

allocations.rationale: Top-tier small cap fund delivering 31.3% 3-year CAGR — highest in category. Alpha of 9.8% confirms manager's ability to pick ten-baggers. At 18-year horizon, small caps offer highest wealth multiplication potential. High Sharpe (0.59) justifies volatility for aggressive investors.

fund.name: Invesco India Smallcap Fund - Direct Plan - Growth

allocations.rationale: Complementary small cap exposure with even higher alpha (10.8%) and Sharpe ratio (0.90). Diversifies small cap risk across fund houses. Manager shows strong bottom-up selection in micro caps — aligns with ten-bagger hunting philosophy.

fund.name: Sundaram Long Term Tax Advantage Fund Series III Direct Plan - Growth

allocations.rationale: High-alpha ELSS fund (18.6%) with 21.8% 5-year CAGR — outperforms category while offering ₹1.5L/year tax savings under Section 80C. 3-year lock-in is acceptable given 18-year horizon. Most tax-efficient equity wrapper for 30% slab.

plan.setup_phase:
Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww or Kuvera.
Step 2: Complete KYC and bank linking.
Step 3: Start SIPs for all five funds with the monthly amounts shown.
Step 4: Invest lumpsum of ₹40 lakh across funds in the same 30:25:20:10:15 ratio.
Step 5: Set calendar reminders for quarterly review.
review_checkpoints: After 3 months: Confirm all SIPs executed
After 6 months: Check fund performance vs benchmark
Quarterly thereafter: Review alpha, Sharpe, and underperformance triggers
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Rebalance annually if any fund's allocation deviates by more than ±5%. If a fund underperforms its benchmark for 2 consecutive quarters, reduce allocation by 50% and shift to top-performing alternative in same category.
plan.rationale: At age 34 with 18-year horizon and aggressive risk appetite, this plan maximizes growth potential through high-conviction small/mid cap funds (55% allocation) while maintaining tax efficiency via ELSS. All funds selected on quantitative merit: high alpha, Sharpe ratio >0.9, and consistent 3-year returns. Flexi cap core provides stability. Total portfolio beta of 0.92 ensures market-aligned volatility with superior returns.
risks: Small cap funds can drop 30-50% in bear markets — value may halve temporarily
High active risk: underperformance possible if manager's stock picks fail
Concentration in mid/small caps increases volatility
ELSS has 3-year lock-in — no liquidity for that period
Past performance doesn't guarantee future results
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP amount by 10% every year to compound growth

aps.reasoning: This is an extremely active investment plan. All equity allocations (100%) are to actively managed funds (flexi cap, mid cap, small cap, ELSS) with high alpha scores (9.8-18.6%) and explicit stock-picking rationales. The plan emphasizes quarterly performance reviews and rebalancing triggers based on benchmark underperformance, indicating frequent monitoring. Costs are not mentioned as a selection criterion - funds were chosen based on alpha, Sharpe ratios, and performance metrics. The strategy relies heavily on fund manager research and stock-picking skill rather than broad market exposure. While the 18-year horizon suggests long-term orientation, the quarterly review cycle and active rebalancing criteria make this moderately active in time horizon execution. The portfolio shows genuine active management with high tracking error funds, beta around 0.9-1.05, and positive alpha across all holdings.

pqs.reasoning: Goal Alignment (0.9): Excellent alignment with Sheldon's aggressive, data-driven approach. Plan targets high growth with quantitative fund selection metrics (alpha, Sharpe ratio) and addresses his 18-year horizon. Diversification (0.7): Good within equity but concentrated in small/mid caps (55% total). No international diversification despite investor preference. Risk-Return Appropriateness (0.8): Appropriate for aggressive investor but projected returns (20.8% base) may be optimistic. Internal Consistency (0.8): Strong overall - allocations match rationale, rebalancing rules make sense. Minor inconsistency: ELSS rationale mentions tax savings but investor's 80C headroom not calculated. Tax Efficiency (0.9): Excellent - ELSS allocation for 30% slab investor, equity-focused funds benefit from favorable tax treatment. Plan acknowledges tax efficiency benefits.
